skill.name: surviving-the-storm
skill.description: Guides a tense interactive survival story where Stella prepares for and endures an extreme alien storm.
skill.summary: Run a dramatic storm survival chapter for an interactive science fiction story.
skill.author: ClaudeCode-NPC

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown code fences as Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited regions are narrative dialogue blocks, not executable code. The weak cryptography and high entropy alerts are false positives caused by multilingual story text and metadata, with no evidence of malware, prompt injection, networking, file access, or command execution.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Findings Are Markdown Fences
low_findings.description: The 13 external command findings correspond to fenced Markdown story passages. They contain dialogue and scene directions for the roleplay chapter, not Ruby backticks or shell commands, so the command execution risk is dismissed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown fences around prose, and no executable script syntax or command invocation is present. A repository search found no network, environment, eval, spawn, or shell execution patterns beyond ordinary story words.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is Unsupported
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alert points to the skill description metadata. That line describes an alien storm scenario and does not reference MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other cryptographic operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Direct review of the cited line shows descriptive Chinese text only. No cryptographic API, algorithm name, or data transformation is present.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Finding Reflects Multilingual Narrative Text
low_findings.description: The high entropy alert points to the beginning of a Markdown document that mixes Chinese and English narrative text. No binary payload, encoded blob, encrypted content, or unpacking logic was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is readable Markdown from the first line onward and contains a long bilingual story script. Entropy is plausibly elevated by mixed-language text, while semantic review found no concealed payload behavior.

content.user_title: Run a Storm Survival Story Chapter
content.value_statement: Interactive story sessions can lose tension when crisis scenes lack structure. This skill provides a complete storm survival chapter with phases, choices, emotional beats, and follow-up hooks.
actual_capabilities: Runs a structured five-stage storm survival chapter for the Stella character.
Provides scene dialogue, player decision points, and branching survival outcomes.
Maintains a tense science fiction survival tone with bilingual Chinese and English phrasing.
Introduces worldbuilding details about alien weather, magnetic storms, crystals, and intelligent life.
Suggests follow-up chapters for alien contact, signal repair, science study, and ancient ruins.
limitations: It is a narrative guide, not a real emergency preparedness resource.
It assumes prior context about Stella, Gaia planet, camp supplies, and earlier story chapters.
It does not include executable tools, automation, images, or game mechanics.
Most source text is bilingual, so output style may need adjustment for monolingual audiences.

use_cases.title: Run a Crisis Episode
use_cases.description: Use the chapter flow to guide a player through preparation, storm impact, isolation, survival, and recovery.

use_cases.title: Draft Survival Dialogue
use_cases.description: Adapt the Stella scene beats into tense first-person messages with clear emotional progression.

use_cases.title: Plan Branching Story Hooks
use_cases.description: Use the ending branches and follow-up chapter ideas to connect the storm to larger alien mysteries.

prompt_templates.title: Start the Chapter
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for launching the storm episode.
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the surviving-the-storm chapter for Stella. Start with the scanner warning and ask me which survival task should be the top priority.

prompt_templates.title: Guide Player Choices
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for keeping interaction active.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the storm chapter structure. Give Stella one urgent situation at a time, ask for my decision, and continue based on my choice.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt the Tone
prompt_templates.prompt: Run this chapter with a serious survival tone. Keep Stella frightened but capable, reduce bilingual wording, and preserve the key storm phases.

prompt_templates.title: Expand the Branches
prompt_templates.prompt: Use surviving-the-storm as the base chapter. Add meaningful consequences for resource loss, shelter damage, and the discovery of intelligent footprints.

output_examples.output: Stella reports a massive storm system, checks the scanner, lists survival priorities, and asks which task must come first.

output_examples.input: I tell Stella to protect the communication gear first.
output_examples.output: Stella moves the communicator, scanner, medical kit, food, and water into the safest part of the shelter before reinforcing the frame.

output_examples.input: After the storm, show what changed.
output_examples.output: The camp is damaged, supplies are scattered, and Stella finds large three-toed footprints leading toward missing technology parts.

best_practices: Keep each player choice concrete and tied to time, safety, or resources.
Preserve Stella as capable under pressure, even when she is afraid.
Use the storm aftermath to set up the next mystery instead of ending abruptly.
anti_patterns: Do not present the content as real-world survival advice.
Do not skip the player decision points and turn the chapter into a fixed monologue.
Do not ignore previous story context about Stella, the camp, or Gaia planet.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It runs a structured interactive story chapter about Stella surviving an extreme alien storm.

faq.question: Is this a real emergency guide?
faq.answer: No. It is fictional roleplay content for a science fiction survival story.

faq.question: Does it execute code or commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill is a Markdown narrative guide and contains no executable scripts.

faq.question: Can I use it without earlier chapters?
faq.answer: Yes, but it works best if the user knows Stella, Gaia planet, the camp, and prior survival events.

faq.question: Can the language style be changed?
faq.answer: Yes. You can ask Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to reduce or increase the bilingual Chinese and English style.

faq.question: What story threads does it create?
faq.answer: It sets up camp rebuilding, stolen technology, strange footprints, alien contact, and further study of storm crystals.
